fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Carolina Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity south carolina, how to transfer physical therapist license to south carolina, physical therapist compact south carolina, south carolina physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: South Carolina Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
board.parent_dept: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)
board.mailing_address:
SC Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation
Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
PO Box 11329
Columbia, SC 29211-1329

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in South Carolina.

steps.title: Create an SC LLR Online Services Portal Account & Apply
steps.description: Register for an account on the SC LLR Online Services Portal and complete the online application for a Physical Therapist license. Pay the required non-refundable application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your accredited physical therapy program to the South Carolina Board of Physical Therapy Examiners.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided during the online application process to complete the required criminal background check.

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) and pass the NPTE. Ensure your scores are sent directly to the SC Board. For endorsement applicants, proof of passing may be verified through licensure verification from other states.

steps.title: Verification of Licensure (for Endorsement Applicants)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, arrange for official verification of all current and past physical therapy licenses held in other states/jurisdictions to be sent directly to the SC Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a South Carolina physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $145. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does South Carolina have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. South Carolina participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in South Carolina without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in South Carolina
hero.dek: Applicants for a Physical Therapist license in South Carolina must apply through the SC LLR Online Services Portal, which handles the entire application process.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account: Go to the SC LLR Online Services Portal, click on "Register an Account," and follow the prompts to create your user ID and password. Once registered, log in to access the application system.
